exemplar.title: To what extent does The Tortured Poets Department by Taylor Swift romanticize mental health disorders?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does The Tortured Poets Department by Taylor Swift romanticize mental health disorders?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus.
Research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused, and consistently connected to the analysis.
Methodology employs a suitable range of primary and secondary sources with recognizable MLA citation style.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks an explicit rationale for source selection and discussion of research procedures.
Formatting inconsistencies in citations detract from methodological polish.
Occasional wording lapses and undeveloped sections (e.g., the Keats subsection) interrupt the flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors for the 5-6 band in topic clarity and research question focus, communicating purpose accurately and maintaining alignment throughout. The methodology is generally robust, employing close reading alongside secondary commentary, but falls short of the highest standard due to uneven scholarly depth, lack of explicit selection rationale, and minor citation glitches. On balance, a holistic score of 5 reflects a strong performance with room for refinement.

strengths: Demonstrates solid knowledge of Romantic poetry and Taylor Swiftâs discography with coherent contextual understanding.
Terminology (e.g., symbolism, allusion) is used mostly accurately and consistently.
Effective use of primary sources such as Verlaineâs original text enhances engagement with foundational literature.
weaknesses: Scholarly source use is patchy, relying frequently on journalistic or web-based material.
Minor lapses in terminology (e.g., âmorning dressâ vs. âmourning dressâ) affect historical accuracy.
Core concepts (e.g., âaccursed poetâ) are defined but not fully integrated into subsequent analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits clear understanding of subject-specific terminology and concepts, situating both Romantic poems and Swiftâs songs within appropriate frameworks. Knowledge of the topic is good but not excellent, as the application of scholarly sources is uneven and some critical concepts are underutilized. This aligns with the upper range of the 3-4 band, warranting a score of 4.

strengths: Research draws on a broad and relevant mix of primary texts, academic articles, and cultural data, maintaining consistent relevance to the RQ.
Analysis features thoughtful line-by-line comparisons (e.g., Poeâs âThe Ravenâ vs. Swiftâs lyrics) and effective imagery discussion.
Argument develops logically across sections, culminating in clear conclusions and acknowledgment of limitations.
weaknesses: Analytical conclusions are occasionally asserted without sufficient supporting evidence or counter-perspectives.
Critical evaluation is present but sometimes brief or contradictory (e.g., romanticization as both problematic and necessary).
Theoretical frameworks and methodological critiques (e.g., Spotify metrics reliability) are suggested but not fully integrated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submissionâs research is âgoodâ¦mostly relevantâ with strong comparative readings and logical argument progression, matching the 7-9 band. Although analysis and evaluation are competent and mostly supported, they lack the sustained critical depth of the highest band. A holistic score of 8 reflects solid critical thinking with room to strengthen evidentiary support and integrate alternative interpretations.

strengths: Includes all required EE structural elements (title page, word count, abstract-style introduction, clear headings, conclusion, Works Cited).
Works Cited format is prominent and consistent, aiding navigation.
Overall organization mirrors the Table of Contents and supports reader comprehension.
weaknesses: Formatting glitches are evident (run-together URLs, inconsistent indentation, missing italics).
Table of Contents alignment issues and occasional layout inconsistencies distract the reader.
A dedicated Methodology subheading and tighter transitions would improve clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation satisfies IB structural requirements and maintains coherent organization, placing it within the 3-4 band. However, visible formatting errors and minor layout inconsistencies prevent a top-band score. A holistic score of 3 accurately reflects competent presentation with identifiable areas for improvement.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student employs a generally appropriate literary-comparative method: close reading of song lyrics and music-video imagery alongside canonical Romantic poems, supported by secondary commentary.  A reasonable range of primary and secondary sources is cited, but scholarly depth is unevenâmany sources are journalistic or web-based, and there is no explicit rationale for source selection or discussion of research procedures.  Citations follow a recognisable MLA style but contain formatting inconsistencies.  These characteristics align best with âmostly complete methodologyâ¦some evidence of informed selectionâ in the 3-4 band, meriting the upper value of 4.

Formatting conventions (word count, page numbers, heading hierarchy, in-text citations, Works Cited) are present and broadly consistent with MLA style.  Nevertheless, there are visible formatting glitches (run-together URLs, occasional inconsistent indentation, missing italics) that detract from full accuracy.  The work therefore earns a solid but not perfect score within the 3-4 band, set here at 3.

The bibliography lists a mixture of primary texts, academic articles, news outlets, and museum websites.  While not uniformly scholarly, the sources are largely pertinent to the RQ (Romantic poetry, Swiftâs lyrics, cultural impact).  Each section draws on these references, showing that research is mostly relevant and purposeful.  The breadth and pertinence fit the âgoodâ¦mostly relevantâ descriptor (7-9 band); limitations in academic rigour temper the mark to the bandâs lower value of 7.

The introduction clearly presents the broad areaâthe Romantic trope of the âaccursed poetââand links it explicitly to Taylor Swiftâs album.  The student then states a precise focus (âTo what extent doesâ¦romanticize mental-health disorders?â) and the subsequent sections are sign-posted in a Table of Contents that matches the discussion.  Occasional wording lapses (âsingersongwriterâ) do not impede clarity.  Overall, the topic and its purpose are communicated accurately and with evident focus, satisfying the descriptors of the 5-6 band, though not with the exceptional polish that would justify the very top of that range.

The argument follows a logical sequence (history of trope â song-by-song comparisons â cultural impact) and reaches a clearâif somewhat equivocalâanswer.  Evaluation of implications is present but tends to be brief and sometimes contradictory (romanticization is âproblematicâ yet ânecessaryâ).  Limited critical weighing of evidence and absence of sustained alternative viewpoints keep the discussion within the competent but not sophisticated âgoodâ¦partially criticalâ range, warranting 4.

Analytical paragraphs do more than describe: they compare Swiftâs lyrics line-by-line with Poe, Coleridge, and Keats, and connect video imagery to historical treatments of schizophrenia.  However, many conclusions (âThis can be problematicâ; âmay be interpreted as encouragementâ) are asserted rather than fully evidenced, and there is limited engagement with counter-perspectives or theoretical frameworks.  Thus analysis is âgoodâ¦mostly supported,â aligning with the mid-point of the 4-6 band.

The essay includes all required EE structural elements: title page with word count, abstract-style introduction, clear section headings, conclusion, and Works Cited.  The internal organisation broadly mirrors the Table of Contents, and transitions are generally coherent.  Minor redundancies and occasional digressions prevent a seamless flow, but the structure clearly satisfies IB requirements, placing it in the 3-4 band at the top value of 4.

The essay demonstrates solid knowledge of both Romantic poets and Swiftâs discography, accurately summarising works such as Poeâs âThe Ravenâ and Keatsâs âNightÂ­inÂ­gale.â  References to streaming statistics and contemporary reception show awareness of cultural context.  Nonetheless, the use of scholarly sources is patchy and some literary points remain surface-level, preventing the work from reaching the âexcellentâ standard.  This places it comfortably in the 3-4 band at the upper end.

Terminology such as âpoÃ¨te maudit,â âRomanticization,â âspeaker,â and âsymbolismâ is applied largely correctly, and literary-critical concepts (allusion, tone, symbolism) are used with reasonable accuracy.  Minor lapses (âmorning dressâ for âmourning dressâ) and informal phrasing occur, but overall usage is mostly accurate and consistent with the 3-4 bandâs expectations.

The research question is explicitly stated in the header and revisited in both introduction and conclusion.  Throughout the body, the discussion consistently returns to whether Swiftâs texts âromanticizeâ mental illness, demonstrating a clear alignment between RQ and analysis.  Although the scope is somewhat ambitious for a single EE (31 tracks plus cultural impact), the question remains sufficiently narrow to sustain a focused argument.  Hence it meets the âclearly stated, focused and effectively connectedâ descriptor in the 5-6 band.

annotations.comment: The Keats section begins with a topic sentence but abruptly ends. Ensure the analysis is fully developed before moving to the next subsection.
The parallels drawn between Keatsâs âOde to a Nightingaleâ and Swiftâs âI Hate It Hereâ effectively highlight shared symbolism of escape and creative inspiration.
The title concisely articulates the studyâs focus on the Romantic trope within Taylor Swiftâs album, establishing clear purpose and relevance.
Including the word count on the title page confirms adherence to the 4 000-word limit requirement and enhances the documentâs professional presentation.
The research question is explicitly stated and well integrated into the introduction, providing a focused line of inquiry that guides subsequent analysis.
Table of Contents items are clear and logically ordered, but pageânumber alignment shifts slightly across rows. Consider adjusting cell widths for consistent alignment.
The introductory paragraph effectively situates the album within Romantic tropes, but lacks a brief statement of the literaryâcomparative methodology to clarify research approach.
The phrase âsingersongwriterâ is merged without hyphens. Ensure subjectâspecific terms (e.g. âsingerâsongwriterâ) are formatted correctly for clarity.
Although the research question is revisited in body paragraphs, adding a dedicated âMethodologyâ subheading before source discussions would improve clarity and focus.
The discussion of Harknessâs comparison is descriptive but could be enriched by explicitly mapping song imagery to poetic devices used by Romantic poets.
The section on accursed poets defines core concepts but does not consistently reference these definitions in subsequent analysis. Integrate term use for coherence.
The analysis of âHow Did It End?â uses several lyric quotes but omits discussion of counterâinterpretations. Briefly consider alternative readings to deepen critical insight.
The comparative reading of Poeâs âThe Ravenâ with Swiftâs âChloe or Samâ¦â is thoughtfully executed, demonstrating effective lineâbyâline analysis.

The linkage between Coleridgeâs albatross symbolism and Swiftâs âThe Albatrossâ is insightful; however, the conclusion that it encourages poor mental health needs supporting evidence or audience reception data.
The reference to a âVictorian morning dressâ appears intended as âmourning dress,â which alters the symbolic meaning. Correct this to maintain historical accuracy.
The musicâvideo analysis compellingly connects visual references to artist Clara Bow, but would benefit from citation of visual studies literature to support interpretative claims.
This detailed discussion of âFortnightâ insightfully captures the tropeâs cyclical nature in mental health depiction, reinforcing the accursed poet framework.
In evaluating cultural impact, integrating a brief critique of data sources (e.g., Spotify metrics) would enhance the argumentâs methodological rigor.
The link to Gracie Abrams as influenced by Swift strengthens the cultural impact section through concrete artist testimony.
Incorporating Swiftâs NYU commencement speech adds depth by showing her own reflections, but consider directly linking her advice back to the research question.
The final reflection acknowledges subjectivity and limitations, which is excellent; consider explicitly summarizing key findings in relation to each Romantic trope discussed.
The Works Cited header is formatted prominently and consistently, aiding reader navigation and reinforcing scholarly presentation.
The Aguasaco citation lacks publisher and location details, reducing source traceability. Include full publication information to meet IB citation standards.
List indents and line spacing in the Works Cited vary across entries. Apply uniform hanging indents and consistent spacing to improve formatting accuracy.
The Johnson entry in the bibliography is cut off midâURL; ensure each reference is complete and accurately transcribed to facilitate source verification.
Stray editorial text âeditmore vertâ appears within citations. Remove or relocate this to avoid confusion and maintain professional presentation.
The inclusion of primary sources such as Verlaineâs original 1888 text demonstrates excellent engagement with foundational Romantic literature.
